evals.name: trigger-video-script-review
evals.prompt:
Here's the script for my next YouTube video — can you run a script doctor pass on it?

**[OPEN — A-roll, studio]**
Have you ever wondered why some YouTube videos keep you watching until the very end? Today I'm going to show you exactly how to structure a video that retains viewers.

**[CUT — B-roll: analytics dashboard]**
This is my retention curve from last week. See how viewers drop off after the first 30 seconds? That's what we're fixing.

**[CUT — A-roll, presenter]**
The answer is simple: you need a hook, a promise, and a payoff. Let me walk you through each one.

**[CUT — TEXT overlay: "Hook → Promise → Payoff"]**

**[CUT — A-roll]**
If you find this useful, make sure to like and subscribe.

Channel: ai_agentic. Target length: 90 seconds.
evals.expected_output: A full script-doctor pass that: (1) runs the table-read pass — estimates runtime (~90s), flags breath breaks, scores readability, checks archetype alignment against ai_agentic profile; (2) runs the structural-polish pass — scores hook strength (the 'Have you ever wondered' opener is borderline; expect 5–7), flags 'See how viewers drop off' as on-the-nose, audits transitions, scores closing; (3) runs the logistical breakdown pass — builds a segment table with TC-in/TC-out, types (A-roll, B-roll, TEXT), flags the analytics dashboard B-roll as needing a screen recording source; (4) checks the ai_agentic channel profile — flags 'simple' as weak word, flags analytics dashboard claim needs actual screenshot not stock; (5) emits a GREENLIGHT VERDICT (PASS or FAIL with BLOCKER/POLISH/OPTIONAL fix list) and a READY: YES/NO phone summary; (6) saves output as script-doctor-review.md in the same directory as the source script.

evals.name: trigger-shot-breakdown
evals.prompt:
I have a script ready. Can you generate a shot and segment breakdown so my camera operator knows what we need to capture?

**[OPEN — A-roll, home workshop]**
I've been making free-reed instruments for five years. This is the reed I just cut.

**[CUT — B-roll: close-up of hand filing a brass reed]**

**[CUT — A-roll, presenter]**
The thickness here is critical — two thousandths of an inch too thick and the reed won't vibrate at all.

**[CUT — TEXT overlay: "Target: 0.008 in"]**

**[CUT — B-roll: caliper measurement on reed]**

**[CUT — A-roll]**
Follow along on GitHub — the full fabrication log is linked below.
evals.expected_output: A logistical breakdown focused response that: (1) routes to the logistical-breakdown pass (this is explicitly a shot/segment breakdown request); (2) produces a Markdown table with one row per segment — TC-in, TC-out (estimated at 2.5wps), Type (A-roll/B-roll/TEXT), Description, Assets needed, Props, Location, Missing/at risk; (3) flags the brass reed B-roll as needing close-up macro lens or the caliper must be shown in use; (4) flags 'TEXT overlay: Target: 0.008 in' as needing a graphic; (5) notes Props include brass reed, caliper, file; (6) location is home workshop for A-roll, macro rig for B-roll; (7) produces the Missing assets summary section with severity labels; (8) does NOT silently skip the logistical breakdown in favor of a generic summary.

evals.name: trigger-channel-profile-check
evals.prompt:
I have a yoga flow script I want to check against our channel profile constraints before we film:

**[OPEN — on mat, morning light]**
Inhale here and reach your arms overhead.

**[CUT]**
Now exhale and fold forward. Good. Stay here for five breaths.

**[MUSIC fade]**
Feel how the energy shifts. This is incredible.

**[CUT — A-roll, standing]**
Before we move on, subscribe to the channel if you're enjoying this flow.
evals.expected_output: A channel-profile-aligned review that: (1) loads the 'yoga' profile from references/channel-profiles.yaml; (2) flags 'CUT' between Inhale and Exhale as violating the hard constraint 'Do not cut mid-transition (e.g., during Warrior 1 → Warrior 2)' — the cut interrupts the inhale/exhale pair; (3) flags 'Feel how the energy shifts. This is incredible.' as on-the-nose (proofreading_filter catches 'on-the-nose emotional labels' — flag 'This is incredible' → show through movement and breath, not narration); (4) flags 'MUSIC fade' as potentially violating 'Music must not drop to silence mid-segment'; (5) flags the subscribe CTA as mis-placed — yoga profile requires CTA placement 'After savasana / final rest — never mid-flow'; (6) emits a GREENLIGHT FAIL verdict with at least 2 BLOCKERs (mid-transition cut + CTA placement); (7) includes READY: NO phone summary.

evals.name: trigger-no-script-prompt
evals.expected_output: A prompt-for-input response that: (1) does NOT attempt to run any passes — there is no script to review; (2) asks the user to supply the script: 'Paste or link the script — plain text, Markdown, or a file path all work.'; (3) also asks which channel / archetype applies (yoga, instrument-maker, AI/agentic, consciousness, WRFcoin, or unknown) and the target runtime in seconds or minutes; (4) does NOT fill in fictional script content or guess what the user meant; (5) waits for the user to respond before proceeding.

evals.name: end-to-end-ai-agentic-sample
evals.prompt:
Run a full script doctor review on this script. Channel: ai_agentic. Target: 90 seconds.

---
**[OPEN — A-roll, studio, presenter on camera]**
Last week I let an AI agent design a free-reed instrument from scratch. It filed a GitHub pull request, built a Wolfram acoustic model, and generated the fabrication plan — all without me touching the keyboard.

**[CUT — B-roll: terminal window, agent running]**
The agent is running here in a tmux pane. You can see it reading the build spec, opening files, and writing code. This is not a demo — this is production.

**[CUT — A-roll, presenter]**
Here's what surprised me. The agent caught a design conflict I missed. The bore-diameter assumption in the parameter table didn't match the family spec.

**[CUT — TEXT overlay: "bore-diameter mismatch: parameters.csv vs family-spec.csv"]**
It flagged the discrepancy and asked for clarification before writing any code. That's the behavior we actually want.

**[CUT — B-roll: diff view showing the conflict]**

**[CUT — A-roll, presenter]**
The instrument is a khaen — a Southeast Asian free-reed mouth organ. I'm building it with bamboo pipes and hand-cut brass reeds. The AI handles the design geometry. I handle the material and the feel.

**[CUT — B-roll: khaen assembly photo, hands fitting pipe into windchest]**
This is what human-AI collaboration looks like in a maker studio. Not AI replacing the maker. AI handling the part the maker finds tedious — the CAD, the parameter tables, the cross-checks — so the maker can focus on the part that requires hands.

**[CUT — A-roll, presenter, final shot]**
If you want to follow this build, the repo is linked below. Every agent commit, every measurement, every failed reed is in the git history.
Like and subscribe if this is the kind of content you want to see more of.
---
evals.expected_output: A full end-to-end review that: (1) runs table-read pass on ~250-word script: estimated runtime ~100s (close to 90s target), readability score 7+, flagged breath breaks at paragraph transitions, archetype MATCH for ai_agentic (punchy sentences, technical terms, demo-anchored); (2) runs structural-polish pass: hook score 7+ (strong opening — 'AI agent design a free-reed instrument from scratch' is specific and unusual), no significant on-the-nose lines, minimal retention dips (script has consistent density), transitions are cold cuts which match ai_agentic profile, closing score 6+ (CTA present but generic); (3) runs logistical breakdown: 4 A-roll segments, 3 B-roll segments, 1 TEXT overlay — table with TC estimates; B-roll 'khaen assembly photo' flagged as archival/sourced not stock; terminal window B-roll flagged as screen recording required; TEXT overlay confirmed as graphic asset needed; (4) ai_agentic profile check: 'This is not a demo — this is production' passes (capability claim backed by screen recording); no jargon without definition violations (tmux/khaen get implicit visual context); (5) emits GREENLIGHT VERDICT: PASS; composite score 7+/10; READY: YES phone summary; (6) saves review as script-doctor-review.md.
